title: Benford's Law Analysis for Journal Entry Fraud
dataSources: `index=erp` general-ledger journal lines for Benford-leading-digit forensic analytics. **SAP S/4HANA or ECC FI:** `sourcetype=sap:fi_journal` via Splunk Add-on for SAP (RFC/IDoc or governed extraction architecture) **or** Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) JDBC queries against **BKPF** (document header: company code `BUKRS`, document type `BLART`, posting date `BUDAT`, user `USNAM`) and **BSEG** (line items: amounts `DMBTR`/`WRBTR`, posting key, GL account `HKONT`, item text `SGTXT`). **Oracle E-Business Suite GL:** `sourcetype=oracle:gl_entry` via DB Connect to **`GL_JE_LINES`** joined to **`GL_JE_HEADERS`** (amounts, JE source, ledger, creator, accounting date). **Canonical Splunk fields** (alias in `props` if vendor names differ): `amount`, `posting_date`, `company_code`, `document_type`, `user_id`, `line_item_text`. **Volume:** ~100K–1M journal entries/month typical for mid-size enterprise GL—design summary indexing or partitioned DB inputs for quarterly Benford reruns.
app: **Splunk DB Connect** `splunk_app_db_connect` ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) **3.x+** installed on Heavy Forwarder or Search Head **where JDBC executes** (`$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/splunk_app_db_connect/`). Optional **Splunk Add-on for SAP** deployed per your SAP integration standard (Splunk Enterprise deployment server class `sap_hf`) when it natively emits `sap:fi_journal`. **Oracle path:** DB Connect Oracle driver + read-only DB user to GL tables; **Heavy Forwarder** recommended to keep DB credentials off interactive SHs. Alternative paths: nightly **CSV**/Dataflow bulk drop to **`monitor://`** with UF if DB Connect is not approved—preserve same sourcetypes for SPL portability.
spl:
index=erp sourcetype IN ("sap:fi_journal","oracle:gl_entry") amount>0
| eval first_digit=substr(tostring(abs(amount)),1,1)
| where match(first_digit, "^[1-9]$")
| stats count as observed by first_digit
| eventstats sum(observed) as total
| eval expected_pct=round(log(1+(1/tonumber(first_digit)),10)*100, 2), observed_pct=round((observed/total)*100, 2), deviation=round(observed_pct-expected_pct, 2)
| where abs(deviation) > 3
| sort first_digit
description: Measures the frequency of leading digits on posted journal amounts and contrasts each digit's share against the classical Benford expectation, surfacing unnatural spikes or gaps that often accompany fabricated round numbers or scripted fraud.
value: Internal audit and forensic accounting get a prioritized short list of ledger populations worth deeper sampling before filings and external reviews, which tightens deterrence against fraudulent postings and strengthens regulatory storytelling with quantitative red flags.
implementation: Exclude reversing entries or zero-net batches if they distort counts; stratify by company code or business unit before global stats; widen sample windows to quarterly for low-volume subsidiaries; corroborate hits with creator ID and duplicate-invoice hunts.
visualization: **Row 1 (full width):** Clustered column chart comparing `observed_pct` vs `expected_pct` for leading digits **1–9** with Benford reference line overlay. **Row 2 (left):** Sortable statistics table (`first_digit`, `observed`, `expected_pct`, `observed_pct`, `deviation`, `N`). **Row 2 (right):** Drilldown donut or treemap of **Materiality**: share of populations by `company_code` flagged vs clean. **Row 3 (full width):** Time series of rolling-window **population N** vs max **|deviation|** to expose sudden posting mix shocks (M&A, system migration). Drilldown from any panel opens **event list** with masked `line_item_text` until analyst role asserted.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application-specific general-ledger payloads, not a normalized data model accelerated for universal fields.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Financial Services
Nigrini — Benford's Law: Applications for Forensic Accounting
ACFE — Fraud Examiners Manual (Benford's Law / fraud analytics)
SAP Help — FI Posting Keys and Document Types

knownFalsePositives:
(1) **Small-sample statistical noise** — When N per entity per window falls below audit materiality (often a few hundred lines), classical Benford and MAD intervals widen and innocent randomness mimics fraud spikes. Mitigation: enforce minimum-N gates in SPL, lengthen the window to a quarter, or aggregate to group level; never page on micro-subsidiaries without analyst override.

(2) **Recurring vendor / subscription payments** — Catalog merchants (rent, SaaS, utilities) often cluster on the same leading digit because tariffs are policy-flat, not because of manipulation. Mitigation: join **vendor master** and exclude known fixed-fee SKUs via `vendor_exclusions.csv`, or stratify Benford inside **discretionary expense** naturals-only populations.

(3) **Rounding and whole-number posting policies** — Some shared-service centers round accruals to whole currency units by procedure, artificially depressing digit entropy. Mitigation: document the control, filter `amount=round(amount,0)`, or exclude those document types (`document_type`) after controller sign-off; pair with substantive journal entry testing.

(4) **Inter-company clearing and netting batches** — High-volume symmetrical pairs net to patterns that distort digit mixes even when reconciled cleanly. Mitigation: tag `business_area`/`trading_partner` and remove intercompany GL accounts (`lookup ic_accounts`) prior to histogram generation; rerun on **third-party-only** subsets for external fraud focus.

(5) **Currency revaluation and translation runs** — Month-end FX revaluation injects correlated amounts driven by rate tables, not behavioral fraud. Mitigation: exclude automated `document_type` codes linked to **FX** and **CTA** runs; run Benford on **functional-currency native** amounts before translation if books permit.

(6) **Post-acquisition ledger merges** — Consolidating disparate charts of accounts mid-period shifts digit mixtures without misconduct. Mitigation: reset baselines post-migration and suspend alerts for **two closes** while Finance publishes new mix benchmarks.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud 9.2+ Search Head with rights to the governed general-ledger index (this document uses `index=erp`) and permissions to create alerts, saved searches, lookups, and dashboards; a **Heavy Forwarder** or **Search Head** that can run **Splunk DB Connect** ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) 3.x **or** a certified **SAP** extraction path that lands `sourcetype=sap:fi_journal`.
• Read-only technical accounts to SAP **BKPF/BSEG** (or appropriate CDS/SQL views) or Oracle E-Business Suite **GL_JE_LINES** / **GL_JE_HEADERS**, with agreed batch or CDC windows; JDBC drivers installed under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/splunk_app_db_connect/drivers/` on the node that executes the connection.
• Field contract in Splunk: `amount` (signed or absolute per policy—this alert expects positive posted amounts after your exclusion filters), `posting_date`, `company_code`, `document_type`, `user_id`, `line_item_text`, and stable sourcetype values (`sap:fi_journal` from Splunk Add-on for SAP or DB Connect; `oracle:gl_entry` from DB Connect to Oracle GL). Without these, leading-digit statistics and drilldowns will be hollow.
• Internal Audit / Controllership sign-off on **materiality**: minimum population **N** per stratum (commonly ≥500 lines per `company_code` per window) before Benford is interpreted; documented exclusion populations (petty cash, subscription SKUs, intercompany wash, treasury instruments) maintained in a lookup reviewed quarterly.
• A **baseline “clean” quarter** (or synthetic clean extract) for tuning the `abs(deviation) > 3` gate and, if you mature the program, calibrating **Mean Absolute Deviation (MAD)** and chi-square companion tests that senior reviewers may request.
• Capacity planning: mid-size enterprises often index **~100K–1M GL journal lines per month**; plan summary indexing or pre-aggregation if interactive 90-day scans exceed your search SLA or fair-share.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**Option A — Splunk Add-on for SAP** (when your organization standardizes on vendor sourcetypes): install on a **Heavy Forwarder** per SAP integration architecture; ensure FI journal events include amount, posting date, company code, document type, user, and line text. Map `_time` to **posting** time when ingestion lag would otherwise distort month-end cutoffs; use `DATETIME_CONFIG` in `props.conf` if timestamps arrive as epoch or regional strings.

**Option B — DB Connect → SAP BKPF/BSEG**: create a **dbxquery** input with parameterized SQL selecting header keys joined to line amounts. Typical rising column pattern: concatenation of `CPUDT`+`CPUTM` plus `BELNR`/`BUZEI` surrogate, or a change log table if SAP replication tools expose it. Landing: `index=erp`, `sourcetype=sap:fi_journal`, execution every 15–60 minutes for detective control, or nightly for retrospective audit. Normalize amounts to functional currency **before** Benford scoring if multi-currency books are merged without translation—otherwise digit distributions reflect FX artifacts, not behavior.

**Option C — DB Connect → Oracle EBS**: join `gl_je_lines` to headers; map ledger, legal entity, creator, JE category equivalents to `company_code`, `document_type`, `user_id`; compute `amount` consistently (debit−credit convention). Same index/sourcetype naming discipline.

Minimal `props.conf` hints (adapt to KV/CSV/JSON payloads):
```
[sap:fi_journal]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 999999
```
Add `EVAL-*` aliases in **props** if SAP field names (`dmbtr`, `bukrs`, `blart`, `usnam`) must appear as canonical `amount`, `company_code`, `document_type`, `user_id`.

**Verification SPL** after first sync:
```
index=erp sourcetype IN ("sap:fi_journal","oracle:gl_entry") earliest=-7d
| eval amt_ok=if(isnotnull(amount) AND tonumber(amount)!=0,1,0)
| stats count, sum(amt_ok) as with_amt,
    dc(company_code) as companies,
    earliest(posting_date) as min_pd, latest(posting_date) as max_pd
```
Chase rows where `amt_ok`=0 separately; confirm **`posting_date` span** aligns with ERP close calendars.

Exclude **automatic reversals**, **rounding-only adjustments**, or **duplicate net-zero batches** upstream when they inflate counts without fraud signal—implement as `WHERE` clauses in DB Connect SQL or Splunk `search` filters agreed with Finance.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=erp sourcetype IN ("sap:fi_journal","oracle:gl_entry") amount>0
| eval first_digit=substr(tostring(abs(amount)),1,1)
| where match(first_digit, "^[1-9]$")
| stats count as observed by first_digit
| eventstats sum(observed) as total
| eval expected_pct=round(log(1+(1/tonumber(first_digit)),10)*100, 2),
     observed_pct=round((observed/total)*100, 2),
     deviation=round(observed_pct-expected_pct, 2)
| where abs(deviation) > 3
| sort first_digit
```

Understanding this SPL
Benford’s law predicts the proportion of counts whose leading digit is *d* as log10(1+1/d). The pipeline enforces strictly digits **1–9** (excluding zero-leading strings and scientific edge cases handled upstream). **`stats`** forms the empirical histogram; **`eventstats`** injects total count so each digit row can express **observed_pct**. **`expected_pct`** is the theoretical benchmark; **`deviation`** is the actionable gap. The **three percentage-point** threshold is pragmatic for first-line monitoring—often coupled with MAD scoring in companion searches: compute MAD classification per Nigrini bands and attach to the dashboard for auditors. **`amount>0`** focuses on disbursement-like patterns—if **your policy** mandates inclusion of reversals, remove that filter but expect digit 1 dominance from credit notes. **`substr` on abs(amount)** handles negative postings without flipping digit logic. For **subsidiaries** with habitual **round thousands** postings (construction progress claims), stratify `(first)` using `company_code`, `business_unit`, or `document_type` *before* `stats`; global blending hides entity-level fraud. Optionally `| bin posting_date span=1mon | stats … by first_digit, posting_month` reveals **acute** distortions timed to quarter-end adjustments.

Schedule as Alert: **cron** `30 6 * * 1` (weekly), time range **`-30d@d` to `@d`**, trigger **Number of results > 0**, **throttle** 7 days on a token combining `splunk_server` + `savedsearch_name` unless you stratify alerts per entity (then throttle by lookup key `company_code`). For **near-real-time** programs, mirror a **daily** summary to `summaryindex=erp_summary` via `sitop`/`collect` patterns and alert from the lightweight summary instead of raw reruns across millions of rows.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Reconcile three postings** randomly sampled from SPL output against SAP FB03 / Oracle JE inquiry with screenshots stored in audit workpapers.
(b) **Digit extraction unit test**: `| makeresults | eval amount=8734.21 | eval first_digit=substr(tostring(abs(amount)),1,1)` must yield `8`; repeat across decimals and scientific notation if `tonumber()` is mandated first.
(c) **Population discipline**: encapsulate strata with `| eventstats count as N by company_code` before alerting; strata with **N < policy minimum** append `| eval verdict="below materiality"` and exclude from alert via `where`.
(d) **Forensic replay**: import a sanctioned historical month tied to prosecuted or substantiated manipulation; demonstrate the search surfaces exaggerated digit deviations or complements MAD thresholds.
(e) **Threshold sensitivity**: simulate raising/lowering `%` gate and record expected annual alert volume curve for Model Risk / Audit Committees.
(f) **Access control**: confirm **field-level masking** works—restricted users see histograms without `user_id` while investigations role retains granular drilldown datasets.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: **four panels** in a Dashboard Studio layout—**Panel 1 (top, full width)** column/bar combo chart of `observed_pct` vs overlaid `expected_pct` by `first_digit`; **Panel 2 (left)** sortable table of `first_digit`, `observed`, `expected_pct`, `observed_pct`, `deviation`, and note `N`; **Panel 3 (right)** treemap or heatmap of max |`deviation`| by `company_code` for the same window; **Panel 4 (bottom)** time series of weekly `N` and peak |`deviation`| to catch **sudden** population mix shifts. Default preset **Last 30 days**; executive clone uses **Last quarter** WITHOUT line-level tokens.

Alerting: **SOAR** playbook “Ledger Benford” ingesting SPL results JSON; **PagerDuty** only for deviations affecting **regulated reporting entities**; **Teams** FYI channel for forensic accounting triage twice weekly when non-empty.

Runbook:
1. Triage analyst opens alert, verifies `N` meets materiality lookup.
2. Export top contributing `document_type`, `user_id`, and `company_code`; pivot with duplicate-invoice SPL packs.
3. Query approval workflow IDs for JE categories flagged.
4. If unexplained statistical deviation persists after Finance narrative, escalate to Fraud Investigation lead with SAP/Oracle exports.
5. Add benign explanatory population to **`benford_exclusions.csv`** with expiry date and reviewer.
6. Quarterly: rotate alert window overlap with continuous controls testing.
7. Annually: re-baseline MAD bands after major M&A ledger merges.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **`first_digit` all `1`** — Investigation often reveals **scaled-down** decimals or truncation to millions; reintroduce precision or filter immaterial micro-amounts.

• **Every digit breaches threshold simultaneously** — Check **currency translation** double-counting or mixed sign conventions; normalize with `| eval amount=abs(tonumber(amount))` only after finance confirms sign semantics.

• **Empty results despite visible skew in charts** — Scheduled search **time zone** misaligned with `posting_date` extractions; align with `TZ` in `props` or use explicit `strptime` on `posting_date`.

• **Runtime skew / slow searches** — Move raw pull to **summary** using nightly `| stats count by first_digit, company_code, _day` into `summary` index; alert searches summary only.

• **Legitimate digit 9 surplus** — Often **telecom / utility** refund patterns; refine strata by vendor category via `line_item_text` tokenization or master-data join.

• **Role errors for analysts** — `srchIndexesAllowed` missing `erp`; fix via authorization role, not ad-hoc `admin` grants.

• **Science vs audit expectations** — When lawyers challenge Benford, attach **ACFE** & **Nigrini** references from the UC references list and provide parallel **random sampling** plan from the same alerted population.


Optional maturity — MAD, chi-square, and stratified baselines
Mature forensic analytics programs rarely stop at percentage-point deltas. Add a companion saved search that computes **Mean Absolute Deviation (MAD)** between observed and theoretical Benford digit proportions using the formulas in Nigrini’s references, then emits a categorical `benford_class` (“close,” “acceptable,” “marginally acceptable,” “nonconforming”) auditors can cite directly in workpapers. Where sample sizes exceed a few tens of thousands of lines per stratum, a **Pearson chi-square goodness-of-fit** transformation can be surfaced as supplemental evidence—but train reviewers that naive chi-square p-values inflate with immense N and should be interpreted alongside practical materiality thresholds, not mechanical p<0.05 paging. Operationalize stratification by tagging each GL line using `document_type`, `business_area`, clearing account flags, currency, posting key families, or user channel (RFC vs batch-ID) prior to aggregation; rerun Benford independently on those strata so treasury noise does not cloak fraud in discretionary operating expenses. Persist nightly summarized counts (`| bucket _time span=1d | stats sum(count) by first_digit, company_code, _time`) into a **summary index** or KV-backed lookup so investigative reruns operate on summarized facts instead of reraw millions of ledger lines at interactive speed. Maintain a regression harness in lower environments that injects synthetic postings with known manipulated digit profiles to prove parser tweaks did not degrade detection.

SOX evidence, lineage, and model risk narration
Treat the Splunk artefacts as regulatory evidence objects: immutable job IDs (`sid`), search string versioning in Git-linked saved searches, and hash-stamped extracts delivered to auditors. Tie each alert escalation to controls language mapping **COSO**, **SOC** themes, or local banking supervisor expectations referencing **quantitative anomalies** requiring human investigation. Coordinate with Model Risk Management if thresholds feed automated ticketing that might influence substantive decisions; document rationale for `abs(deviation) > 3` vs MAD bands, reviewer override rights, and exception ratification through the control owner committee. Maintain **master data lineage** showing how BKPF/BSEG or Oracle JE columns normalize into Splunk canonical fields (`amount`, `posting_date`, etc.). When finance migrates ERP chart versions mid-year, annotate “blackout resets” preventing false narrative that fraud surged purely because numbering schemes shifted.

Operational analytics beyond the headline SPL
Standing **panels** tying Benford outliers to complementary signals reduce analyst dead-ends without changing the sanctioned detection SPL stored in `spl`. Drilldown searches may join **`user_travel_cards`**, segregation-of-duties matrices, dormant-vendor lookups, procurement PO matches, duplicate-invoice lexical similarity, weekend posting counts, unusual **posting key** combos, repeated **line_item_text** strings, velocity of manual journals by **`user_id`**, and segregation conflicts between **`user_id`** and **approval** surrogate fields maintained in lookups. Operationalize recurring **tier-two** hunts that replay `first_digit`-biased populations through **Isolation Forest**, **logistic scores**, or rules engines—but keep each layer’s purpose explicit for examiners skeptical of opaque scores. Integrate archival exports to **`outputlookup forensic_sample_<quarter>`** for legal hold subsets when subpoenas constrain data motion.

Incident response collaboration and privacy
Operationalize RACI tying **Finance Control**, **Internal Audit**, **Fraud Investigations**, **IT Access**, **Legal**, and **DPO/privacy office** triggers when names appear in escalation lists depending on GDPR/GDPR-lite or sector privacy regimes. Pseudonymize `user_id` in broad-access dashboards via reversible tokenization stored in vault-restricted lookups; escalate only vaulted joins for criminal referrals. Maintain chain-of-custody notes when forensic copies traverse SOAR ticketing.

Synthetic parser regression and QA cadence
Schedule quarterly parser regression tests leveraging anonymized excerpts from forensic matters (with approvals) blended into QA indexes; assert expected digit distributions after scripted transforms mimic vendor patch weekends. Align **Splunk Cloud** ingestion rules (INGEST_EVAL) parity tests with Enterprise props to avoid divergence.

Extended troubleshooting contexts
• **Digit 1 dominates after pricing freeze** — Long-term tariff freezes or capped utility reimbursements inflate leading ones; rerun excluding vendor categories via master-data lookups.

• **Digit 8/9 starvation after decimal policy** — Corporations forbidding fractions <0.05 currency units artificially truncate tails; widen tolerance or segregate petty cash.

• **Hour-of-day spikes** — Not fraud but **automated depreciation** postings with identical templated descriptions; corroborate with job scheduler logs referenced from `cron_id` lookups.

• **Cross-border dual posting** — Parallel books in transactional vs functional currency inadvertently indexed twice inflates N inconsistently across digits—dedupe on composite keys (`company_code`, `belnr`, `buzei` analogs).

• **Cloud data residency** — When EU ledgers replicate to US Splunk stacks, coordinate transfer impact assessments prior to alerting on EU-natural entries.

Search performance, workload management, and indexer routing
Route `index=erp` to **warm** buckets sized for 13-month rolling windows typical of continuous monitoring programs; schedule Benford saved searches in **workload management** pools labelled `continuous_monitoring` so ad-hoc exploratory queries from analysts do not starve automated runs. For petabyte-scale estates, run the heavy `stats` stanza on **indexer parallelization** with `allow_old_summaries=false` when reading from summary indexes to avoid stale overlaps. If `eventstats` pushes memory limits, materialize two-stage searches: stage A writes `collect` or `summary` output per `company_code`; stage B merges.

Quantitative storytelling for leadership
Translate digit deviations into dollar **exposure estimates** by multiplying over-represented digit counts by average line size for that digit bucket and comparing to quarterly **restatement** risk appetite statements. Pair each leadership readout with narratives that Benford is probabilistic and never dispositive—pairing with concrete transaction reviews wins trust.

Inter-company, tax, and consolidation overlays
Finance organizations running **SAP EC-CS**, **SAP Group Reporting**, Hyperion consolidation, or similar close tools often replicate elimination entries alongside local ledgers inside the same Splunk sourcetype. Without tagging **consolidation layer** origins, benign adjustments that mechanically repeat templated decimals can spoof fraud-like digit gaps. Operationalize ingestion metadata (`source_system`, `ledger_group`, `version`) and drive Benford principally off **single-entity statutory** ledgers flagged in master data before rolling up. Tax provisioning feeds (ASCII block closes, VAT settlement runs) routinely inject clustered amounts tied to percentages—exclude `document_type` codes mapping to recurring tax automatons after tax signs off quarterly. When **capitalization** thresholds move (lease accounting updates), expect digit-shape shifts correlated to policy—not fraud—for two closes; annotate those windows in dashboards so audit committees contextualize anomalies.

Executive dashboard annotations and RACI tagging
Panels should display **effective control owner**, **frequency**, **exceptions YTD**, and **prior-year comparison** MAD bands simultaneously so board packs remain comprehensible without opening Splunk. Automate watermarking Splunk-exported PNGs bound for external counsel with Bates-like identifiers stored in **`evidence_manifest`** lookups.

Drilldown SPL hygiene and command safety
Encourage analysts copying drilldowns into ad-hoc workspaces with `format` settings that avoid accidental `join` explosions; cap `head` in training materials to teach targeted sampling. Sandbox roles should lack `delete` on indexes to prevent mis-clicks during frantic quarter-end triage.

Continuous control attestation tie-in
Map each alert outcome to **SOC1/ISAE3402** narratives or equivalent user-entity controls stating management reviews quantitative journal anomalies. Maintain **retention** aligning with statutory periods (often 7–10 years); export cold logs to compliant object storage with Splunk **SmartStore** if on-prem indexers face disk pressure.

Training curriculum for finance partners
Host annual Benford literacy sessions covering **when** the law fails (simulated uniform pricing), **how** Splunk samples differ from Excel ad-hoc pulls, and **why** manual follow-up remains mandatory. Record attendance for audit evidence.
grandmaExplanation: We count how often amounts in our books start with a one versus a nine, because honest messy numbers follow a predictable pattern. When that pattern suddenly looks too neat or lopsided, we dig in before someone's cooking the books hurts customers and regulators.
